title: Dash n Splash
tagline: Dash n Splash — heritage city of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Dash N Splash was a water theme park located in Chennai, India.
overview.description: Dash N Splash was a water theme park located in Chennai, India. It was opened in 1995 and was the first exclusive water park in Chennai and South India. The idea of opening the water park was to get rid of the heat, sweat and dust of the city. The motto of the water park was "Refresh Yourself".

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Thandalam, Chennai
topPlaces.description: Thandalam is an emerging location of the Chennai city in the Sriperumbudur taluk of Kancheepuram district, Tamil Nadu, India. It is in the National Highway 4 (NH-4) or State Highway 84 (SH-84) at Sira Bypass.

topPlaces.name: Queens Land
topPlaces.description: Queens Land is a theme park in Poonamallee, Chennai, India, covering 70 acres (28 hectares). It opened in August 2003.

topPlaces.name: Irungattukottai
topPlaces.description: Irungattukottai is a fast emerging neighbourhood of Chennai, strategically on the Chennai-Bangalore Highway (NH4). It is approximately 34 km from downtown Chennai.

topPlaces.name: Madras International Circuit
topPlaces.description: The Madras International Circuit (MIC, previously known as Madras Motor Race Track and Irungattukottai Race Track) is a permanent motor racing circuit located in Irungattukottai, Chennai, India. It was built in the late 1980s and was inaugurated in 1990.

topPlaces.name: Kuthambakkam Mofussil Bus Terminus
topPlaces.description: Kuthambakkam Mofussil Bus Terminus is a bus terminus located in Chennai, India, providing inter-state bus transport services. It is located on the Chennai–Bengaluru Highway (NH 48) .

topPlaces.name: Chembarambakkam Lake
topPlaces.description: Chembarambakkam Lake is located in Kancheepuram district, Tamil Nadu, India, about 25 km (16 mi) from Chennai. It is the biggest of the two important rain-fed reservoirs, the other one being the Puzhal Lake, that supply drinking water to Chennai city .

topPlaces.name: Thirumazhisai
topPlaces.description: Thirumazhisai is a western suburb of Chennai, India. It is located in Thiruvallur district of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Jagannatha Perumal temple
topPlaces.description: Jagannatha Perumal Temple (also called Tirumazhisai) Temple is dedicated to Hindu god Vishnu in Tirumazhisai, Chennai City, Tiruvallur district, in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Jagannatha Perumal and his consort Lakshmi as Thirumangaivalli.

items.note: Thandalam is an emerging location of the Chennai city in the Sriperumbudur taluk of Kancheepuram district, Tamil Nadu, India. It is in the National Highway 4 (NH-4) or State Highway 84 (SH-84) at Sira Bypass.

The Madras International Circuit (MIC, previously known as Madras Motor Race Track and Irungattukottai Race Track) is a permanent motor racing circuit located in Irungattukottai, Chennai, India. It was built in the late 1980s and was inaugurated in 1990.
Kuthambakkam Mofussil Bus Terminus is a bus terminus located in Chennai, India, providing inter-state bus transport services. It is located on the Chennai–Bengaluru Highway (NH 48) .

Chembarambakkam Lake is located in Kancheepuram district, Tamil Nadu, India, about 25 km (16 mi) from Chennai. It is the biggest of the two important rain-fed reservoirs, the other one being the Puzhal Lake, that supply drinking water to Chennai city .
Thirumazhisai is a western suburb of Chennai, India. It is located in Thiruvallur district of Tamil Nadu.

Jagannatha Perumal Temple (also called Tirumazhisai) Temple is dedicated to Hindu god Vishnu in Tirumazhisai, Chennai City, Tiruvallur district, in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Jagannatha Perumal and his consort Lakshmi as Thirumangaivalli.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Dash n Splash?
faq.a: The best time to visit Dash n Splash is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Dash n Splash?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chennai International Airport (18 km) and the nearest railway station is Chennai Central (29 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Dash n Splash?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Dash n Splash at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Dash n Splash?
faq.a: Stays in Dash n Splash start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Dash n Splash?
faq.a: The top attractions are Thandalam, Chennai, Queens Land, Irungattukottai, Madras International Circuit, Kuthambakkam Mofussil Bus Terminus.
